violations.text: The door to the outside, located in the back, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New door sweep was added, but it was not tight fitting. New Violation. Correct By: 01/21/2025

The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by new droppings in various locations. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/21/2025
The non-food contact surfaces of the lids, unused plates, shelving in various locations throughout the establishment have an accumulation of rodent droppings. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/21/2025
The walls in the back storage area is in poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/21/2025

The floors in various locations in the facility have an accumulation of rodent droppings/grease. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/21/2025
Allergy statement is lacking on the menus. Per PIC allergy statement will be added once new menus are printed. New Violation. Correct By: 01/07/2025

Various foods were observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. New Violation. Correct By: 01/07/2025
Several blocks of rodenticide were observed stored on the floor. Rodenticide to be removed. Rodenticide blocks shall be placed by professional pest control only. New Violation. Correct By: 01/07/2025

Several ROP seafood items were observed thawing with package in tact. ROP seafood must be removed from ROP environment before thawing. Items were still frozen. Items were removed from package. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/07/2025

The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by a large accumulation of droppings throughout the establishment. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/07/2025

The door to the outside, located in the back, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/07/2025
The non-food contact surfaces of the various shelving/ lids have an accumulation of rodent droppings. All areas to be cleaned and sanitized. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/07/2025
The walls in the dry storage area is in poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/07/2025

The floors and walls throughout the facility have an accumulation of soil residue, rodent droppings, and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/07/2025

Various food product was observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 11/20/2023

The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by droppings throughout facility. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 11/20/2023
Inspector observed a towel being used to cover rice. FOOD shall be protected from contamination that may result from a factor or source. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/20/2023
Single service items are not being stored properly in their original protective package and/or are stored on a surface that is not clean in the sushi area. New Violation. Correct By: 11/20/2023

The following non food contact surfaces have an accumulation of debris: 1.shelves throughout facility observed with soil and food residue, other debris, and/or rodent droppings. 2.hoods from stove ventilation has accumulation of grease and soil residue. 3.all refrigeration units interior and exterior have accumulation of soil and food residue. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 11/20/2023

The floors under/behind equipment throughout the facility and walk in cooler have an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 11/20/2023
The ceilings throughout facility is in poor repair(observed holes). The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 11/20/2023
